Distributed Tensorflow
docs.title: Home

docs.text:
Distributed Tensorflow :


First, lets see a simple example of running multiple tasks using distributed tensor flow.


# test.py
jobName = sys.argv[1]
taskIndex = sys.argv[2]
worker = ['localhost:2221', 'localhost:2222']
cluster = tf.train.ClusterSpec({
worker
: worker})
# Create and start a server for the local task.
server = tf.train.Server(cluster, job_name=jobName, task_index=taskIndex)
if jobName == 'worker':
    W = tf.Variable(1, trainable=False, dtype=tf.int32, name='W')
    init_op = tf.global_variables_initializer()
    with tf.Session() as sess:
    sess.run(init_op)
    for i in range(10):
        incr = tf.assign(W, W + 1)
        print sess.run(incr)
        time.sleep(3)




python test.py worker 0
python test.py worker 1
docs.title: Dtf

docs.text:
First, lets see a simple example of running multiple tasks using distributed tensor flow.  # test.py
jobName = sys.argv[1]
taskIndex = sys.argv[2]
worker = ['localhost:2221', 'localhost:2222']
cluster = tf.train.ClusterSpec({ worker : worker})
# Create and start a server for the local task.
server = tf.train.Server(cluster, job_name=jobName, task_index=taskIndex)
if jobName == 'worker':
    W = tf.Variable(1, trainable=False, dtype=tf.int32, name='W')
    init_op = tf.global_variables_initializer()
    with tf.Session() as sess:
    sess.run(init_op)
    for i in range(10):
        incr = tf.assign(W, W + 1)
        print sess.run(incr)
        time.sleep(3)  python test.py worker 0
python test.py worker 1
